summary: Large gmregclear decompilation PR. It replaced many gmregclear placeholders with C, expanded local state/layout knowledge, and promoted several header/type signatures. decomp.dev reported +3292 matched bytes, six new full matches, and many near-match improvements; the remaining regressions were explicitly attributed by the author to data locality pending linking.

key_files.role: Main decompilation work: new C bodies, local structs, state layouts, UI/model/text handling, spawn/record logic.
Promoted many gmregclear prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures and added a needed forward include.
Improved shared gm type information: callback signatures, float fields, and a newly exposed u16 field in lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t.

what_changed: Added the includes needed by the new C bodies, including baselib gobj/jobj/aobj/mobj/sislib/controller headers, gm_1601/gm_1A36/gm_1B03/gmregcommon, interface headers, and lblanguage.

Expanded gmregclear state structs: lbl_80472D28_t now exposes JObj, GObj, ImageDesc, Archive, DynamicModelDesc, HSD_Text, counters, and byte flags; lbl_80472E48_t grew from size 0x80 to 0x90 with a matching STATIC_ASSERT; lbl_80472ED8 was retyped from padding into PlayerInitData, RegClearSpawnEntry arrays/pointers, counters, and flags.
Added local data/layout helpers RegClearSpawnEntry, RegClearCharEntry, RecordBlock, fn_8017FA1C_arg, bitfield overlays, and the u16 joint/table array lbl_803D8B88.

Implemented gm_8017CE34 as a large StartMeleeData setup routine using UnkAdventureData callbacks, rule flags, PlayerInitData initialization, CPU/color setup, special stage/rule cases, and gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable.

Implemented or improved character/opponent helper logic in fn_8017D9C0, gm_8017DB88, and fn_8017DD7C, including random character selection using HSD_Randi, sentinel value 0x21, callback-driven character attributes, and a corrected fn_8017DD7C signature.
Implemented match-end flag routines fn_8017E8A4 and fn_8017EE40 using MatchEnd data, stage lookups, time checks, player slot/character checks, and packed bit writes to _x448.

Implemented clear/decision screen UI and model functions around fn_8017F2A4, fn_8017F47C, fn_8017F608, fn_8017FA1C, fn_8017FBA4, fn_8017FE54, fn_8017FF1C, fn_801803FC, and fn_80180630, including HSD_SisLib text creation, language-specific SdDec/SdClr assets, GmRegClr archive loading, JObj animation, controller navigation, and score/count display updates.

Implemented distance/display and related flow functions fn_80180C60, fn_80181598, and fn_80181708, with digit animation, timer hiding, audio triggers, player-freeze calls, and IfHrNoCn model setup evidence in nearby code.

Implemented spawn and record handling functions fn_80181C80, fn_80181E18, gm_80182174, gm_80182578, fn_80182B5C, and gm_80182DF0, using lbl_803D8D08-backed table data, mode cases 0x21-0x26/33-38, sentinel 0x3E7, and RecordBlock score/icon/time layouts.

Updated gmregclear.h prototypes for many previously unknown routines, including gm_8017CE34, fn_8017D9C0, gm_8017DB88, fn_8017DD7C, fn_8017F1B8, fn_8017F2A4, fn_8017F47C, fn_8017F608, fn_8017FA1C, fn_8017FBA4, fn_8017FF1C, fn_801803FC, fn_80180C60, fn_80181598, fn_80181708, fn_80181C80, and gm_80182578.
Updated types.h so Unk1PData.xC.x24 entries use f32 for x4/x8, UnkAdventureData callback fields x48/x50/x58 have concrete function pointer signatures, and lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t exposes xE at offset 0xE.
smart_moves: Converted large anonymous/padded globals into offset-annotated partial structs instead of trying to fully semantically name every field at once; this made many later offsets usable while preserving uncertainty with xNN/pad_NN names.
Kept unstable helper layouts local to gmregclear.c, such as RecordBlock, RegClearSpawnEntry, RegClearCharEntry, and fn_8017FA1C_arg, avoiding premature public API commitments.
Used typed callbacks in UnkAdventureData so gm_8017CE34 and gm_8017DB88 could call table-driven difficulty/color/float functions without opaque UNK_T casts.
Used bitfield overlay structs for packed flags in MatchEnd._x448 and lbl_80472E48.x0, making bit writes explicit while still matching narrow byte storage.
Modeled repeated record-table blocks with RecordBlock where useful, but retained direct lbl_803D8D08 offsets in gm_80182DF0 where the function's data access pattern required it.
Documented the data-ordering problem in the PR discussion instead of obscuring it: the author stated that regressions were due to data locality and would be solved when linking.
decomp_lessons: When a file is blocked by many unknown callbacks, promoting shared type signatures in types.h can unlock large local matches; here UnkAdventureData callback types and Unk1PData float fields were important.
For state-heavy game-mode files, expose fields by offset and add STATIC_ASSERTs as soon as size evidence is known; lbl_80472E48_t's updated 0x90 size is a useful pattern.
Local bitfield overlays are a practical way to express packed result/flow flags without globally renaming unknown bytes too early.

Header cleanup should follow decompiled bodies immediately, but remaining unchanged UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS entries are a useful follow-up signal when implementation evidence exists but public signatures are still uncertain.
Clear/results UI code often needs exact HSD_SisLib calls, language-asset branches, JObj traversal, and text positioning; matching may depend on preserving awkward temp variables and call order.
Data-locality regressions can appear even when code matching improves substantially; record them explicitly and revisit after linking/data-order work rather than treating them as semantic failures.
naming_conventions: Address-based symbols remain for unknown functions: gm_8017CE34, fn_8017F608, gm_80182DF0, etc.
Offset-style field names are used for partial layouts: x20, x84, xC0, x6A4, pad_30, pad_6BD.
Local helper structs use descriptive but still conservative names: RegClearSpawnEntry, RegClearCharEntry, RecordBlock, fn_8017FA1C_arg.
Public prototypes in gmregclear.h keep address comments such as /* 17CE34 */ and use concrete types only where the diff had evidence.
Sentinel/value conventions visible in this slice include character sentinel 0x21, spawn-table sentinel 0x3E7, and record/mode cases 0x21-0x26 / 33-38.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK after several functions or returns to shape stack size, including examples with 8, 0x18, 0x24, 0x30, and 0x48 byte pads.
Used #pragma dont_inline around matching-sensitive functions such as fn_8017E8A4, fn_8017EE40, fn_8017F14C, and fn_80181BFC.
Preserved narrow casts and signedness details, especially u8/s8/s32 casts around character IDs, rule flags, callback returns, and PlayerInitData fields.
Used pointer arithmetic into StartMeleeData/PlayerInitData arrays where struct typing alone did not express the original access pattern.
Used volatile local storage in fn_80181C80 to influence stack/register allocation.

Kept matching-shaped control flow where visible, including goto/label style in fn_80182B5C, unreachable PAD_STACK markers after returns, and repeated/nested HSD_JObjGetChild/HSD_JObjGetNext traversal chains.
Used language-specific branches through lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS for SdDec.usd/SdDec.dat and SdClr.usd/SdClr.dat, preserving asset load behavior and data references.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal reviews were present in the slice.
decomp.dev reported matched code at 64.36%, a +0.08% improvement and +3292 bytes.
decomp.dev listed six new full matches: fn_8017F608, fn_8017FBA4, fn_8017E8A4, fn_8017FA1C, gm_80182DF0, and fn_8017DD7C.
decomp.dev also listed 21 improvements in unmatched gmregclear items, including gm_8017CE34 at 95.96%, fn_80180C60 at 97.58%, gm_80182578 at 96.56%, and fn_8017FF1C at 99.38%.
The report showed one broken match, gm_80182554 dropping from 100.00% to 99.44%, and a .data regression from 4.99% to 0.33%.
Author comment: "Regressions are caused by data locality, will be solved when linking".
follow_up_queries: Recheck gm_80182554 and .data after linking/data-locality work lands.
Promote semantic names for lbl_80472D28_t, lbl_80472E48_t, lbl_80472ED8, RegClearSpawnEntry, RegClearCharEntry, and RecordBlock when gameplay meaning is confirmed.
Audit remaining gmregclear.h UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS entries near functions that now have C bodies, especially fn_80181E18, gm_80182174, and fn_80182B5C.
Investigate the remaining mismatches in gm_8017CE34, fn_80180C60, gm_80182578, fn_8017FF1C, and other near-matches reported by decomp.dev.
Confirm whether RecordBlock can safely replace more direct lbl_803D8D08 offset accesses without hurting matching.
Continue typing UnkAdventureData x5C/x60 and other callback/table fields as additional callsites are decompiled.
